title: Anthropic talks stall as open AI spreads worldwide - TCR 06/16/26
description:
Anthropic flew Tom Brown, Logan Graham, and Nicholas Carlini to Washington and left Monday's Commerce talks with the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export-control shutdown unresolved, the DOJ asked a court to call xAI's 57 unpermitted Memphis turbines a national-security asset to dismiss an NAACP pollution suit, and ALS patient Casey Harrell logged 3,800+ independent hours speaking through an AI brain implant at 99% accuracy.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-16-2026/

0:00 The Anthropic export-control standoff hardens in DC
2:43 DOJ calls xAI's turbines a national-security asset
4:58 A speech brain implant crosses into daily life
7:30 A worker wins a religious exemption from AI
9:31 The preemption push meets a public that wants rules
11:57 Synthesis and closing

This episode covers the Anthropic export-control standoff and the open-letter pushback, the DOJ national-security override of the xAI Clean Air Act case, the first home power user of a speech brain implant, a software engineer's religious exemption from mandated AI, and a Johns Hopkins poll showing over 70% of Americans want AI rules.

The same phrase carried the weight across the day's governance stories: national security. The administration invoked it to keep Claude Fable 5 pulled offline worldwide on the demand that a safety lab strip its own guardrails, and Anthropic's most senior staff flew to Washington and left with nothing resolved, while dozens of researchers signed an open letter calling the order unjustified. The Justice Department pointed the same logic at the physical layer, asking a court to dismiss the NAACP's pollution suit against xAI by calling its Memphis turbines a national-security asset, even as the Southern Environmental Law Center counted 57 units running without permits and nitrogen-oxide emissions up 111% since April. Underneath the override, the people the systems touch wrote the real settlement: a Johns Hopkins poll found over 70% of Americans, including enthusiastic daily AI users, want enforceable rules and a human option, and a North Carolina engineer won a religious exemption from mandated AI. And the medicine that widens human reach kept arriving - Casey Harrell, paralyzed by ALS, has spoken more than 3,800 independent hours through an AI-decoded brain implant at 99% accuracy, while gait-synchronized brain stimulation cut falls for Parkinson's patients across two Nature Medicine trials.

Additional Reading:
